summarization.a: IV curve of three samples, all with different plasma exposure times and/or compositions. The tested solar cells had a MnOx thickness of 20 nm.

a.question: What was the thickness of the tested MnOx layers on the solar cells?
What happens to the short-circuit current when the MoOx layer thickness is doubled?
a.answer: The current decreases in all produced solar cells when the oxide layer thickness is doubled.

a.question: Why might cells with MoOx layers from the 1-second O₂ plasma process show lower efficiency and more pronounced s-shaped IV behavior?
a.answer: Because their lower initial oxygen content can lead to a reduced MoOx work function after ITO sputtering and annealing, increasing the hole transport barrier and worsening VOC and fill factor.

a.question: What feature in the IV curves limits the fill factor and efficiency of the cells?
a.answer: A pronounced s-shape in the illuminated IV curves limits the fill factor and efficiency.
